questions.question: Define a Python class and explain the purpose of the __init__ method with an example relevant to IoT applications.
questions.hint: Think of a class as a template for IoT devices like sensors
keyPoints: Class is a blueprint for creating objects with attributes and methods
__init__ is a constructor method called automatically when an object is created
It initializes instance variables with given parameters
In IoT context, used to initialize sensor properties like ID, type, location
Syntax: def __init__(self, parameters): self.attribute = parameter

questions.question: Explain the concept of inheritance in Python. How does it promote code reusability in IoT system design?
questions.hint: Consider how different IoT devices share common properties
keyPoints: Inheritance allows a class to inherit attributes and methods from another class
Parent class (base class) provides common attributes
Child class (derived class) inherits and can extend functionality
In IoT: Base 'Device' class with child classes 'Sensor' and 'Actuator'
Reduces code duplication and promotes modular design

questions.question: What is encapsulation in Python? How is it achieved using private attributes? Give an IoT example.
questions.hint: Think of protecting critical sensor configuration data
keyPoints: Encapsulation bundles data and methods that operate on data into a single unit
Restricts direct access to some attributes to prevent unintended modification
Private attributes denoted by double underscore prefix (__attribute)
Accessor/mutator methods (getters/setters) provide controlled access
IoT example: Encapsulating sensor calibration data to prevent unauthorized changes

questions.question: Differentiate between class variables and instance variables in Python with an example.
questions.hint: Consider a counter for total devices vs unique ID for each device
keyPoints: Class variables are shared across all instances of a class
Instance variables are unique to each object instance
Class variables defined at class level, outside any method
Instance variables defined inside __init__ using self
Example: device_count (class) vs sensor_id (instance) for IoT devices

questions.question: Explain method overriding in Python. How is it useful in designing IoT device hierarchies?
questions.hint: Consider how temperature sensor and humidity sensor might implement read() differently
keyPoints: Method overriding allows a child class to provide specific implementation of a method already defined in parent class
The method in child class must have same name and signature as parent
Enables polymorphic behavior in class hierarchies
IoT example: Different sensor types overriding read_data() method differently
Promotes flexibility and extensibility in IoT systems

questions.question: What are the different types of inheritance supported in Python?
questions.hint: Map IoT scenarios to each type
keyPoints: Single inheritance: One parent class, one child class
Multiple inheritance: One child class inherits from multiple parent classes
Multilevel inheritance: Chain of inheritance (grandparent -> parent -> child)
Hierarchical inheritance: One parent class, multiple child classes
Hybrid inheritance: Combination of two or more inheritance types

questions.question: Design a Python class hierarchy for an IoT-based Weather Monitoring System. Create a base class 'Sensor' and derive classes for 'TemperatureSensor', 'HumiditySensor', and 'PressureSensor'. Include appropriate attributes, methods, and demonstrate inheritance.
questions.hint: Start with base class and progressively add specialized child classes
keyPoints: Define base class Sensor with attributes: sensor_id, location, unit
Include __init__ constructor with self parameter
Define base method read_data() returning placeholder value
Create TemperatureSensor class inheriting from Sensor
Add temperature-specific attributes and override read_data()
Similarly create HumiditySensor and PressureSensor classes
Demonstrate object creation for each sensor type
Show method invocation demonstrating polymorphism

questions.question: Write a Python program to implement an IoT Device class with proper encapsulation. The class should have private attributes for device ID, status, and battery level. Include methods to get/set status and check battery level. Demonstrate creating device objects and accessing them appropriately.
keyPoints: Define class IoTDevice with private attributes using double underscore
Include __init__ method initializing device_id, status, battery_level
Define getter methods: get_status(), get_battery_level()
Define setter methods: set_status(), set_battery_level()
Include validation in setters (e.g., battery between 0-100)
Create multiple device objects demonstrating usage
Show proper access through methods vs direct access attempt

questions.question: Explain the four pillars of Object-Oriented Programming (OOP). How are these principles applied in developing IoT applications using Python?
questions.hint: Connect each OOP principle to a practical IoT example
keyPoints: Encapsulation: Bundling data and methods, protecting sensor data
Inheritance: Creating specialized device classes from base device class
Polymorphism: Different sensors implementing common interface differently
Abstraction: Hiding complex implementation details of IoT protocols
IoT application: gpiozero library uses classes to represent GPIO pins
IoT application: MQTT client classes for publishing/subscribing
Benefits: Reusability, maintainability, scalability for IoT systems

questions.question: Design a Python class for an IoT Actuator Controller that demonstrates multiple inheritance. Create a 'Controller' base class, a 'Communicator' mixin class, and an 'ActuatorController' class that inherits from both. Include appropriate methods for controlling actuators and communicating status.
questions.hint: Think of combining control logic with communication capabilities
keyPoints: Define Controller class with control methods like turn_on(), turn_off()
Define Communicator class with send_data(), receive_data() methods
Create ActuatorController inheriting from both using comma separation
Include __init__ calling both parent classes using super()
Demonstrate methods from both parent classes
Show how multiple inheritance combines functionalities
Discuss MRO (Method Resolution Order) for multiple inheritance

questions.question: Write a Python program to implement a Smart Home IoT system using classes. Create classes for 'Device' (base), 'Light' (inherits Device), 'Thermostat' (inherits Device), and 'SmartHome' (composition with list of devices). Include methods to add devices, control all devices, and get status reports.
questions.hint: Use composition where SmartHome contains Device objects
keyPoints: Define Device base class with name, status, device_type attributes
Create Light class with brightness attribute and adjust_brightness() method
Create Thermostat class with temperature attribute and set_temperature() method
Define SmartHome class containing list of Device objects (composition)
Include methods: add_device(), turn_all_on(), turn_all_off(), get_status()
Demonstrate creating smart home and adding multiple devices
Show controlling all devices through SmartHome class

questions.question: Write a Python class to simulate an IoT Temperature Sensor that logs temperature readings. The class should maintain a list of readings, add new readings, calculate average, minimum, and maximum temperatures. Demonstrate with at least 5 temperature readings.
questions.hint: Use Python's built-in sum(), min(), max() functions
keyPoints: Define TemperatureSensor class with sensor_id and readings list
Initialize empty readings list in __init__
Define add_reading(temperature) method to append to list
Define get_average() returning sum/len of readings
Define get_minimum() returning min(readings)
Define get_maximum() returning max(readings)
Create sensor object and add readings: [22.5, 23.0, 21.8, 24.2, 23.5]
Display average, min, max values with proper formatting

questions.question: Create a Python class to represent an IoT Gateway with multiple connected sensors. The class should track sensor count, store sensor objects, and provide methods to calculate total data packets generated. Assume each sensor generates packets based on its frequency attribute.
questions.hint: Total packets = sum of (frequency × duration) for all sensors
keyPoints: Define Sensor class with sensor_id and packet_frequency attributes
Define IoTGateway class with gateway_id and sensors list
Include class variable to track total gateways created
Method add_sensor() to append sensor to list
Method calculate_total_packets(duration) summing all sensor packets
Formula: packets = frequency × duration for each sensor
Create gateway, add 3 sensors with frequencies 10, 20, 15
Calculate total packets for 1 hour duration

questions.question: Write a Python program to implement a battery management system for IoT devices using classes. Create a 'Device' class with battery consumption rates and a 'BatteryManager' class that tracks battery levels, alerts when low, and schedules device shutdown.
questions.hint: Track battery percentage and trigger alerts below threshold
keyPoints: Define Device class with name, battery_level, consumption_rate
Define BatteryManager class with devices list and threshold
Method register_device() to add devices to manager
Method update_battery() decreasing battery based on consumption
Method check_low_battery() returning devices below threshold
Method shutdown_low_battery() turning off devices with critical battery
Demonstrate with 3 devices at different battery levels
Simulate battery drain and show alerts

questions.question: What is the difference between 'is-a' and 'has-a' relationships in OOP? Give IoT examples for each.
questions.hint: is-a = inheritance, has-a = containing/using other objects
keyPoints: is-a relationship: Inheritance (specialization), 'TemperatureSensor is a Sensor'
has-a relationship: Composition/Aggregation, 'SmartHome has a Light'
is-a: Used when child class is a type of parent class
has-a: Used when class contains or manages other class objects
IoT is-a: LED inherits from Actuator
IoT has-a: Gateway contains list of Sensor objects
